title: Goo Goo Gai Pan

jokes.text: You drove in with three people on your grill.
jokes.setup: DMV employee questioning someone's driving skills
jokes.punchline: Reveals the extreme extent of bad driving - literally having people stuck on the car
jokes.explanation: Strong visual absurdity with efficient delivery - implies hilariously bad driving without showing it

jokes.text: At least I'm out doing things.
jokes.setup: Being criticized for dangerous driving with people on the grill
jokes.punchline: Dismissive response that completely ignores the severity
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - shows Bouvier twin stubbornness and deflection

jokes.text: This car is cooler than Guy Lombardo.
jokes.setup: Complaint about car being too hot
jokes.punchline: Reference to extremely dated bandleader as comparison point
jokes.explanation: Shows character's outdated cultural references - Guy Lombardo being 'cool' is hilariously wrong

jokes.text: Good heavens, she's some sort of female madman!
jokes.setup: Witnessing erratic behavior
jokes.punchline: The oxymoronic 'female madman' construction
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay mixing gendered language with 'madman' - shows confusion and linguistic awkwardness

jokes.text: Stop that, you want-wit! I might get stung by a bumbled-bee.
jokes.setup: Hot flash causing confusion
jokes.punchline: Mangled versions of 'nitwit' and 'bumblebee'
jokes.explanation: Excellent malapropisms showing heat-induced confusion - 'want-wit' and 'bumbled-bee' are perfectly crafted

jokes.text: Car! - Car what? Car is the subject, but I don't know the verb.
jokes.setup: Emergency situation requiring urgent communication
jokes.punchline: Grammar pedantry in a crisis moment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - grammatical precision during an emergency is perfectly Simpsons-style humor

jokes.text: Those are my lungs!
jokes.setup: Someone thanking God that airbags activated
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'airbags' are actually her lungs
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with efficient delivery - the visual implication is both absurd and anatomically specific

jokes.text: Son, menopause is when the stork that brings babies gets shot by drunken hunters.
jokes.setup: Child asking what menopause is
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely wrong but poetic explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer wisdom - completely wrong but delivered with confidence and unexpectedly poetic imagery

jokes.text: What do you know. She's out of eggs, and if you're watching this, so are you.
jokes.setup: Educational video about menopause
jokes.punchline: Direct address breaking fourth wall to insult viewer
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta humor that breaks format expectations - the casual cruelty is perfectly dark

jokes.text: So let's all give menopause a round of men-applause!
jokes.setup: Upbeat educational video trying to be positive
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun on 'men-applause'
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad pun that works because it's so forced and awkward

jokes.text: 'Men-applause'? I'm not saying that.
jokes.setup: The terrible pun just delivered
jokes.punchline: Actor breaking character to reject the line
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment showing even the actor recognizes how bad the pun is

jokes.text: I didn't want to get pregnant and lose my looks.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she never had children
jokes.punchline: Implies she currently has looks worth preserving
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Selma's vanity despite her appearance is established character trait

jokes.text: All he can do is eat me when I'm dead.
jokes.setup: Discussing how pet iguana can't care for her
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions being eaten by pet
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny with matter-of-fact delivery - the casual acceptance of being eaten is perfectly morbid

jokes.text: I'm not dead, idiot. I know. That was for the other patients.
jokes.setup: Homer saying goodbye as if Selma is dying
jokes.punchline: Homer's 'thoughtful' explanation reveals he was being considerate to others
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's strange logic - being considerate in the wrong way is perfectly in character

jokes.text: This is one problem damaged food can't solve.
jokes.setup: Patty suggesting dented can sale to cheer up Selma
jokes.punchline: Acknowledgment that this problem is beyond their usual coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Great character moment establishing that damaged food is their usual solution to problems

jokes.text: Wow, you really are down.
jokes.setup: Selma rejecting damaged food as solution
jokes.punchline: Recognition that rejecting bargain food means serious depression
jokes.explanation: Understated recognition of how serious the situation is based on established character traits

jokes.text: That foot's not dead. It's just asleep.
jokes.setup: Presumably viewing a concerning medical condition
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of what appears to be a serious problem
jokes.explanation: Implies visual gag of concerning medical condition being dismissed casually

jokes.text: From now on, we're a Skoal family.
jokes.setup: Man saying he'll give up smoking for the baby
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's switching to chewing tobacco instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - appears to be making healthy choice but actually switching to equally unhealthy habit

jokes.text: What she said was, she's tired of having rabies.
jokes.setup: Man explaining he misheard his wife about giving away baby
jokes.punchline: Reveals wife was complaining about having rabies, not babies
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Cletus moment - casual mention of having rabies while the real concern is mishearing

jokes.text: Give me back my belly fruit!
jokes.setup: Demanding his baby back
jokes.punchline: Referring to baby as 'belly fruit'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cletus terminology - 'belly fruit' is hilariously crude and perfectly in character

jokes.text: Give me back my belly fruit!
jokes.setup: Cletus wanting his child back
jokes.punchline: Refers to baby as 'belly fruit'
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully crude and poetic Cletus terminology. 'Belly fruit' is both disgusting and endearing.

jokes.text: Great, another job lost to a foreigner.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting Chinese adoption
jokes.punchline: Homer treating baby adoption as job competition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - applying economic anxiety to completely unrelated situation with great efficiency

jokes.text: No 'hen without cock.'
jokes.setup: Explaining marriage requirement for adoption
jokes.punchline: Using barnyard metaphor that sounds sexually suggestive
jokes.explanation: Cultural miscommunication creating accidental innuendo - works for the innocent delivery

jokes.text: I apologize if that is a double-entendre in your language. It is not in ours.
jokes.setup: The sexual-sounding 'hen without cock' phrase
jokes.punchline: Claiming innocence while clearly aware of the innuendo
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor acknowledging the innuendo while pretending innocence

jokes.text: Oh, I just felt a chill go through my very soul.
jokes.setup: Selma declaring love for Homer (fake husband)
jokes.punchline: Homer's visceral negative reaction to being 'loved' by Selma
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction - the physical revulsion at fake affection is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Yeah, those Sloppy Joes will do that.
jokes.setup: Homer's spiritual chill from Selma's declaration
jokes.punchline: Marge attributing it to lunch instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Marge's practical explanation deflating Homer's dramatic moment

jokes.text: And how about me getting off work by saying I'm teaching math skills to inner city youths?
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how he got time off work
jokes.punchline: Absurdly noble lie that Homer would never actually do
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer lie - grandiose and completely contrary to his character

jokes.text: Yes, there are many kinds of heroes.
jokes.setup: Homer's lie about teaching inner city youth
jokes.punchline: Marge's dry acknowledgment that treats lying as heroic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - supportive but subtly acknowledging the absurdity

jokes.text: Oh, you are a very greedy man. The other dragons shall hear of this.
jokes.setup: Homer's dream negotiation with dragon
jokes.punchline: Dragon's formal, disappointed response to Homer's greed
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist dream logic - dragon society having formal disappointment protocols

jokes.text: Hey, dragon! You fly like a girl!
jokes.setup: Homer in dream with dragon
jokes.punchline: Homer insulting a mythical creature with playground taunts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - reducing epic fantasy to childish insults is perfectly in character

jokes.text: You name it, oh, wise mountain ape.
jokes.setup: Selma asking for a favor
jokes.punchline: Homer addresses Selma as 'wise mountain ape'
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and insulting Homer terminology. 'Mountain ape' is both creative and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: The man who broke a dragon's heart...
jokes.setup: Continuing dream sequence
jokes.punchline: Dragon's ongoing dramatic disappointment in Homer
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the dragon's formal disappointment system

jokes.text: But, Marge, I don't want her to be happy!
jokes.setup: Marge appealing to Homer's compassion for Selma
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest admission of pettiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - brutally honest selfishness delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Okay, don't do it for her. Deal! - Do it for me.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to convince Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer eagerly agreeing to NOT help before hearing the real request
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Homer's eagerness to avoid helping Selma

jokes.text: 'Cause your sweet love is the one thing that saved my life from being an unmitigated disaster.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he'll help Marge
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently admitting his life would be a disaster without her
jokes.explanation: Homer's accidentally sweet but backhanded compliment to Marge

jokes.text: Ha-ha! Now we're even!
jokes.setup: Homer just gave Marge a compliment
jokes.punchline: Homer treating the compliment as settling a score
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - viewing marriage as a transaction where compliments are currency

jokes.text: It makes the Lincoln Memorial look like crap.
jokes.setup: Pilot pointing out General Gao monument
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of American landmark
jokes.explanation: Good cultural comparison joke with efficient delivery

jokes.text: And this is Marge, our surprisingly hot nanny.
jokes.setup: Homer introducing family members
jokes.punchline: Calling his wife the 'surprisingly hot nanny'
jokes.explanation: Clever way to explain Marge's presence while giving her a backhanded compliment

jokes.text: Don't mention it, Miss... October.
jokes.setup: Marge thanking Homer for the compliment
jokes.punchline: Homer making up 'Miss October' as fake name
jokes.explanation: Good improvisation showing Homer making up increasingly elaborate lies

jokes.text: But don't look at her passport-- it's different.
jokes.setup: Homer's lie about Marge being 'Miss October'
jokes.punchline: Preemptively explaining why the passport won't match
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Homer digging himself deeper with unnecessary detail

jokes.text: You can wave your arms at them and make funny faces, and they just have to stand there and take it.
jokes.setup: Learning about Shaolin monks
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete misunderstanding of their discipline
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer cultural ignorance - reducing ancient discipline to playground harassment

jokes.text: Homer, no, stop; you're thinking of Buckingham Palace!
jokes.setup: Homer about to harass the monks
jokes.punchline: Reveals Homer confused monks with British guards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection revealing the source of Homer's confusion - perfectly logical for him

jokes.text: He's like a little angel that killed 50 million people.
jokes.setup: Homer looking at sleeping Mao statue/figure
jokes.punchline: Combining 'little angel' with mass murder
jokes.explanation: Masterful dark comedy - the juxtaposition of 'little angel' with genocide is perfectly horrifying and funny

jokes.text: With these pogo sticks, we'll finally make it over.
jokes.setup: Learning about Great Wall built to repel invaders
jokes.punchline: Homer's ridiculous solution using pogo sticks
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer engineering - absurdly simple solution to ancient military problem

jokes.text: Can't make it Monday. My kid has a thing.
jokes.setup: Director calling for everyone to return with fresh ideas
jokes.punchline: Treating acrobatic performance like office job with family obligations
jokes.explanation: Good mundane response to extraordinary situation - treating circus like corporate job

jokes.text: I'm a Chinese acrobat!
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he can lie about his identity
jokes.punchline: Choosing the most implausible profession for himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer choice - the least believable profession for his body type and abilities

jokes.text: Well, Tibet was considered 'pretty independent.' How'd that work out?
jokes.setup: Lisa claiming she's pretty smart
jokes.punchline: Referencing Chinese occupation of Tibet as comeback
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark political humor - using geopolitical oppression as a casual comeback

jokes.text: Such loving discipline. Maybe someday, someone will love you too, childless servant.
jokes.setup: Marge disciplining Lisa
jokes.punchline: Madam Wu's backhanded compliment/insult to Marge
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Madam Wu. The passive-aggressive insult disguised as compliment fits her officious personality.

jokes.text: Why doesn't Pearl Jam ever come here?!
jokes.setup: Citizen questioning everything after disillusionment
jokes.punchline: Jumping from political oppression to concert tours
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - revolutionary fervor focused on rock band access

jokes.text: Then why am I seeing rudimentary free markets?
jokes.setup: Homer learning they're Communists
jokes.punchline: Homer making surprisingly astute economic observation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - Homer being unexpectedly perceptive about Chinese economic contradictions

jokes.text: No, a porcupine snuck in while you were asleep. Go on, shoo! Come back in year of porcupine. Which is never!
jokes.setup: Homer thinking he's getting acupuncture
jokes.punchline: Doctor revealing it's actually a porcupine, then shooing it away
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with absurd explanation - the zodiac reference adds perfect detail

jokes.text: Without him, I wouldn't have you.
jokes.setup: Introducing Homer as the baby's 'daddy'
jokes.punchline: Technically true but for completely wrong reasons
jokes.explanation: Good situational irony. The statement is literally true but misleading about Homer's actual role in the adoption.

jokes.text: How'd you like a little time with 'Miss October'?
jokes.setup: Selma thanking Homer for his help
jokes.punchline: Offers to pimp out Marge using Homer's earlier fake name
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the Miss October bit. Selma offering sexual favors as payment is appropriately inappropriate.

jokes.text: Fool me once, shame on you. Fool me twice, shame on Wu!
jokes.setup: Classic 'fool me once' saying setup
jokes.punchline: Pun on her own name 'Wu' instead of 'me'
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that works because it's character-specific and fits the situation

jokes.text: Wow, the fortune cookies here really are more accurate.
jokes.setup: Watching bad events unfold
jokes.punchline: Homer treating real tragedy as fortune cookie validation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer observation - treating serious situation through lens of tourist stereotypes

jokes.text: Just think about the day you found that Junior Mint in your belly button.
jokes.setup: Lisa helping Homer look serene for disguise
jokes.punchline: References Homer's disgusting but blissful memory
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer-specific memory. The gross-out factor combined with Homer's joy at finding candy in his body fat is perfect character writing.

jokes.text: Feng shui? I thought that was just a trick to sell crappy end tables to the West.
jokes.setup: Another guard mentioning feng shui
jokes.punchline: Cynical take on feng shui as Western marketing ploy
jokes.explanation: Good cultural satire. The meta-commentary on feng shui commercialization from a Chinese perspective is clever.

jokes.text: Feng shui? I thought that was just a trick to sell crappy end tables to the West.
jokes.setup: Someone mentioning feng shui
jokes.punchline: Chinese person dismissing their own cultural export as Western marketing scam
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural satire - Chinese person cynically viewing their own cultural export

jokes.text: Let's cut him in chunks and worship the chunks.
jokes.setup: Guards struggling to move heavy Buddha statue
jokes.punchline: Suggesting dismembering religious icon for convenience
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about religious pragmatism. The casual suggestion to dismember Buddha for easier worship is wonderfully sacrilegious.

jokes.text: Plus, we get free apple, orange and grapefruit juice.
jokes.setup: Explaining benefits of Delta lounge during crisis
jokes.punchline: Homer focusing on juice selection while fleeing authorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - focusing on airline perks during international incident

jokes.text: This is Wang Pu's time to shine.
jokes.setup: Bureaucratic paperwork requiring notary
jokes.punchline: Minor functionary excited about his moment of importance
jokes.explanation: Good minor character moment. The excitement over bureaucratic relevance fits the episode's bureaucracy theme.

jokes.text: The Heimlich maneuver was invented the very next day.
jokes.setup: Explaining her father died choking on ping-pong ball
jokes.punchline: The cruel timing of life-saving technique being invented too late
jokes.explanation: Masterfully dark timing joke - the cruel irony of medical advancement coming one day too late

jokes.text: As a spoiled American teenager.
jokes.setup: Saying Ling will return to China someday
jokes.punchline: Predicts specific negative American teenage stereotype
jokes.explanation: Standard cultural stereotype humor. Homer's prediction about American teenage behavior is mildly amusing but predictable.

jokes.text: Feast on my shorts, stupid father man.
jokes.setup: Homer noticing something wrong with 'Bart'
jokes.punchline: Formal translation of Bart's catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural translation of Bart's signature phrase - formal version is perfectly awkward

jokes.text: American jerks are going home
jokes.setup: Chinese musical number as Americans leave
jokes.punchline: Blunt insults set to cheerful music
jokes.explanation: Decent contrast between cheerful music and insulting lyrics

jokes.text: When we wake, the world will end.
jokes.setup: Continuing the farewell song
jokes.punchline: Escalating from insults to apocalyptic threats
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from tourist insults to ominous prophecy

tmdb_overview: Selma decides to adopt a child from China, but when the rules say she must be married, Homer must pose as her husband for the fearsome adoption agent Madame Wu.
